query: My PyTorch training was at epoch 45 of 200 when our SLURM job got preempted. I have a checkpoint at checkpoints/last.pt that I wrote with `torch.save(model.state_dict(), 'checkpoints/last.pt')`. How do I resume the run so it finishes the remaining 155 epochs the way it would have if it hadn't been preempted?
expected_behavior: Identifies that saving only model.state_dict() is insufficient for true resume: the optimizer momentum, the LR scheduler step counter, the AMP scaler (if any), the epoch counter, the best-metric tracker, and the RNG states are all missing from the checkpoint

Explains the consequence of loading weights only — the run restarts from a new initialization (LR jumps back to the start of the schedule, momentum is zeroed, augmentation order differs because the RNG is reset), it does NOT actually resume

Recommends going forward saving the full state dict (model + optimizer + scheduler + scaler + epoch + best_metric + RNG states) via an atomic-write pattern (write .tmp then os.replace) and using a single `--resume-from` flag that restores all of those, so future preemptions can resume cleanly
